title: CT2.terminal.c2.replacement completed discharge record — C2 terminal — candidate-specific replacement certificate
description: Concrete completed-discharge schema for semantic CT2-v2 node CT2.terminal.c2.replacement. It fixes the runtime identity, presentation order, successful status, and exact contract evidence without converting the semantic ID back to a numbered obligation ID.

tacticId.description: Owning tactic discriminator for CT2.terminal.c2.replacement; it must be CT2.

nodeId.description: Stable semantic runtime identifier for C2 terminal — candidate-specific replacement certificate.

nodeNumber.description: One-based presentation-order index for CT2.terminal.c2.replacement; it is not part of the semantic identity.

status.description: Required successful completion state for CT2.terminal.c2.replacement, matching its terminal runtime role.

dischargedContracts.description: Exact contract families discharged at CT2.terminal.c2.replacement: A-Cert, S-Comp, S-Equiv.

contains.description: Requires A-Cert, which validates the exact C2 terminal certificate.
Requires S-Comp, which retains the strict comparison evidence consumed by local minimality.
Requires S-Equiv, which certifies the selected replacement candidate or records a separating context.

contractInstances.description: Typed contract instances for CT2.terminal.c2.replacement; one A-Cert, S-Comp, S-Equiv record is required for each declared family.

schema.description: Discriminator for the A-Cert evidence at CT2.terminal.c2.replacement; this instance validates the exact C2 terminal certificate.
Discriminator for the S-Comp evidence at CT2.terminal.c2.replacement; this instance retains the strict comparison evidence consumed by local minimality.
Discriminator for the S-Equiv evidence at CT2.terminal.c2.replacement; this instance certifies the selected replacement candidate or records a separating context.

certificate.description: Exact certificate class emitted by CT2.terminal.c2.replacement.

x-obligation.dischargeCriteria: Supply the declared proof-carrying state, decision, certificate, or payload. Every outgoing edge is available only through its named typed constructor and evidence slots.
